text: ‘Readiness to publish the gospel of peace’; other renderings have been suggested here, but it seems most probable that St Paul, with characteristic independence of his metaphor, is recalling the language of Is. 52.7.

vv. 14-17: Cf. Is. 11.5; 59.17; Wis. 5.19. v. 17: It is not certain whether the clause ‘which is God’s utterance’ refers to the ‘sword of the spirit’, or whether we should print ‘Spirit’ with a capital, and understand St Paul to describe the Holy Spirit as God’s utterance. If we take ‘the spirit’ as meaning ‘the spiritual life’ in general, it is possible that he is telling the Ephesians to arm themselves with the word (or message) of God, which acts upon the spirit like a sword (cf. Heb. 4.12). But the sense of interpreters is greatly at variance over this passage.
Here again it would be possible to print ‘in the Spirit’; if so, ‘keep awake to that end’ might also be rendered, ‘keep watchful in him’.
